front: Painting: massive photomontage; top features Friedrich Ebert, bottom features Kollwitz, Heartfield, Marx, Lenin
back: Cut with the Kitchen Knife (Hannah Höch)
work: Cut with the Kitchen Knife Dada through the Last Weimar Beer-Belly Cultural Epoch of Germany (1919)

front: Painting: includes cutouts of the word 'Dada' and phrases like 'Invest your money in Dada!'
back: Cut with the Kitchen Knife (Hannah Höch)
work: Cut with the Kitchen Knife Dada through the Last Weimar Beer-Belly Cultural Epoch of Germany (1919)

front: Painting: near center, headless dancer throws woman's head toward an elephant
back: Cut with the Kitchen Knife (Hannah Höch)
work: Cut with the Kitchen Knife Dada through the Last Weimar Beer-Belly Cultural Epoch of Germany (1919)

front: Painting: Hindenburg's face on exotic dancer's body; looming image of Wilhelm II
back: Cut with the Kitchen Knife (Hannah Höch)

front: Painting: divided into four sections, one 'anti-Dada'; includes women's suffrage map
back: Cut with the Kitchen Knife (Hannah Höch)

front: Painting: series combining magazine cutouts with photos of ethnographic artifacts
Painting: includes The Sweet One (African statue with flapper's legs)
Artist: lover and collaborator of Raoul Hausmann, creator of Mechanical Head (The Spirit of Our Time)
Painting: depicts a giant gun pointing at a banker whose head is based on a photo of chemist John Herschel
Painting: photomontage of woman with lightbulb head peering over pocket watch; BMW logos in background
Artist: Berlin Dadaist alongside George Grosz and John Heartfield; Nazis branded her work 'degenerate art'
Painting: a soldier on a hill stares at a giant rose atop a tree
Painting: photomontage protesting abortion regulations; mother wearing a mask
